section.title: Opportunities
section.subtitle: Join, Learn, Build, Publish
section.description: Structured research, development, thesis, volunteer, and collaboration pathways for students, developers, researchers, and partners who want to contribute to HUMACH Research.

items.title: Research Internship
items.summary: For students interested in AI, data science, biomedical AI, NLP, signal processing, human-centered computing, or applied computing research.
items.details: Research interns contribute to literature review, dataset preparation, experiment design, metric analysis, paper writing, and research communication. Tasks are matched to the intern's current skills and learning goals.
items.applicationNote: Share your research interests, current skills, and one preferred HUMACH research area.

items.title: Development Internship
items.summary: For students interested in web systems, dashboards, automation tools, databases, AI prototypes, static websites, and GitHub-ready software outputs.
items.details: Development interns help convert HUMACH research ideas into practical interfaces, reusable code, data-driven pages, dashboards, and prototypes. The focus is learning by building real project components.
items.idealFor: Students and junior developers building a practical portfolio
items.applicationNote: Share links to GitHub, portfolio, coursework, or any previous web/software work if available.

items.title: Thesis / Project Supervision
items.summary: For students who need a structured computer science, AI, data science, health AI, or software development project for coursework, capstone, thesis, or portfolio development.
items.details: HUMACH can help shape feasible project questions, define datasets or software outputs, and align the project with research, implementation, and reporting requirements.
items.applicationNote: Include your institution/project requirements, deadline, and preferred topic direction.

items.title: Volunteer Research Assistant
items.summary: For motivated learners who want to support literature collection, dataset organisation, result checking, documentation, visualisation, or research administration tasks.
items.details: This pathway is suitable for early contributors who want to gain research exposure before joining a larger research or development task.
items.applicationNote: Explain your availability and the kind of support task you want to learn first.

items.title: Collaboration / Partnership
items.summary: For researchers, universities, student teams, industry partners, and communities interested in applied AI, computing, health technology, or research software collaboration.
items.details: Collaboration pathways may include joint research, shared datasets, prototype development, student supervision, technical reports, publications, workshops, or external project support.
items.idealFor: Researchers, labs, organisations, and supervised student groups
items.applicationNote: Describe the collaboration idea, expected outcome, timeline, and available resources.

process.title: Express Interest
process.description: Submit the form with your pathway, background, skills, interests, and preferred research or development direction.

process.title: Match a Pathway
process.description: HUMACH maps your interest to a research, development, thesis, volunteer, or collaboration pathway.

process.title: Define Tasks
process.description: A small set of milestones is defined so the work is practical, measurable, and suitable for your current level.

process.title: Build Evidence
process.description: You work toward clear outputs such as a report, code contribution, dashboard, prototype, literature summary, or publication support.
